header: 3Spliting the Identity into Forward and Reverse Processes

images.caption: Figure 2:Overview of forward processes across VP, VE-Karras, and rectified-flow parameterizations (exported from the original interactive visualizationhttps://iclr-blogposts.github.io/2026/blog/2026/rethinking-diffusion-langevin/).
Figure 3:A forward diffusion step with step sizeΔ​t\Delta tadds Gaussian noise to data, pushing samples closer to a Gaussian distribution.
Figure 4:The forward and reverse diffusion processes compose to reproduce Langevin dynamics.
Figure 5:Reverse trajectories under different parameterizations (exported from the original interactive visualizationhttps://iclr-blogposts.github.io/2026/blog/2026/rethinking-diffusion-langevin/).

Figure 6:Part of a forward–reverse diffusion cycle: the last two steps of the forward process (green arrows, increasingtt) followed by the first two steps of the reverse process (blue arrows, increasingt′t^{\prime}while decreasingtt).
Figure 7:Each horizontal row shows a Langevin dynamics step that maps a forward sample𝐱t\mathbf{x}_{t}to a new reverse sample𝐱(T−t)′\mathbf{x}_{(T-t)^{\prime}}from the same probability density.
